checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=61360040.50 (2011-09-30) increased to Goodwill=94864277.62 (2011-12-31), a 54.6% jump in one quarter, while FY2011 revenue grew only 13.0% (SalesRevenueGoodsNet=321776172.03)

OtherAssetsNoncurrent=4322051.95 (2009-12-31) grew to OtherAssetsNoncurrent=5777619.03 (2010-12-31) and OtherAssetsNoncurrent=11026590.38 (2011-12-31), a cumulative +155% vs revenue growth of ~46% over the same span

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-41582783.24 (FY2011) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=37484573.11 (FY2011) - a ~$79M divergence, with OCF positive despite a large net loss
evidence.location: 10-K cash flow statement / income statement, FY2011

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=3457641.56 (FY2009) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=27634342.87 (FY2009), OCF nearly 8x reported net income
evidence.location: 10-K cash flow statement / income statement, FY2009

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=53692863.23 (2009-12-31) to InventoryNet=64043165.59 (2010-12-31) to InventoryNet=71029887.58 (2011-12-31): +19.3% then +10.9%, both below revenue growth of +29.1% then +13.0%

GrossProfit=184092027.98 (FY2009)/SalesRevenueGoodsNet=220472275.15 = 83.5% margin, vs GrossProfit=255033807.61 (FY2011)/SalesRevenueGoodsNet=321776172.03 = 79.3% margin - a modest ~4pp decline, not a sharp inconsistency

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2478333.64 (2009-12-31) fell to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=1531768.55 (2010-12-31), a 38% decline, while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 34803829.32 (2009-12-31) to 44268884.95 (2010-12-31), a 27% increase - allowance coverage ratio fell from 7.1% to 3.5% of AR

AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=15471874.38 (2010-12-31) declined to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=12459535.15 (2011-12-31), a 19.5% decrease, despite SalesRevenueGoodsNet growing from 284705944.40 (FY2010) to 321776172.03 (FY2011) and Assets nearly doubling to 668883378.54 (2011-12-31)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2010-05-26, approximately three months after the original 10-K filed 2010-02-26
Cluster of 8-K filings on 2011-09-21, 2011-09-27, 2011-09-28, 2011-09-29, and 2011-10-07 coinciding with the period of the large Q3 2011 net loss and major asset/liability shifts

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Annual data available from FY2008 through FY2011 for revenue, COGS, gross profit, net income, and cash flow items, plus quarterly data for 2009-2011, filed across 10-K accession 0001193125-12-078937 and prior 10-K/10-Q accessions

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reduction of bad-debt allowance/reserve relative to a growing receivables base, potentially releasing reserves into income and contributing to the disproportionately large Q4 2010 net income spike, which drove most of FY2010 earnings.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2478333.64 (2009-12-31) declined to 1531768.55 (2010-12-31) even as AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 34803829.32 to 44268884.95 over the same period

NetIncomeLoss=36869603.46 (Q4 2010) represented ~79% of full-year NetIncomeLoss=46604936.17 (FY2010), a disproportionate quarterly spike not matched by a comparable revenue jump (SalesRevenueGoodsNet=76977769.25 in Q4'10 vs 71594850.01 in Q3'10)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Large non-cash charge (e.g., acquisition-related impairment, contingent consideration remeasurement, or transaction/restructuring costs) recorded in Q3 2011 in connection with a major acquisition, producing a big net loss quarter without a corresponding operating cash outflow.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-40215324.11 (Q3 2011) drove the full-year loss NetIncomeLoss=-41582783.24 (FY2011), while NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities remained positive at 37484573.11 (FY2011)

Assets=480265697.82 (2011-03-31) surged to Assets=757802751.10 (2011-06-30) then declined to Assets=663665959.36 (2011-09-30), and Goodwill jumped from 61360040.50 (2011-09-30) to 94864277.62 (2011-12-31), consistent with a major acquisition around this period

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential under-accrual of operating expenses/liabilities in FY2011 despite revenue growth and a substantially larger post-acquisition asset base, which would understate expenses and liabilities.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=15471874.38 (2010-12-31) fell to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=12459535.15 (2011-12-31) even as SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew from 284705944.40 (FY2010) to 321776172.03 (FY2011) and Assets grew from 477467079.88 (2010-12-31) to 668883378.54 (2011-12-31)

top_signals: Large FY2011 divergence between net loss (NetIncomeLoss=-41582783.24) and positive operating cash flow (NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=37484573.11), driven by an unexplained -$40.2M net loss in Q3 2011 concurrent with a major acquisition (Assets nearly doubling, Goodwill jumping to 94864277.62)

Declining bad-debt allowance coverage (AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent fell from 2478333.64 in 2009 to 1531768.55 in 2010) even as receivables grew, coinciding with a disproportionate Q4 2010 earnings spike (36869603.46 of 46604936.17 FY2010 net income)

Accrued liabilities declined (AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent from 15471874.38 to 12459535.15) despite revenue growth and balance-sheet expansion in FY2011, plus a 10-K/A amendment and a cluster of 8-K filings around the Q3 2011 loss period
